completed_tasks.name: Fix DataFrameSchemaBackend.validate() to set check_obj.pandera.errors for PySpark
Fix _concat_failure_cases() PySpark dispatch and avoid PySpark materialization in run_check()
Fix dtype string format in ColumnBackend.check_dtype()
completed_tasks.progress: PySpark dtype comparison rewritten (uncommitted) — still failing on string alias cases (int/integer → IntegerType())

completed_tasks.name: Checkpoint: SC2c row-index applicability decision — scope-out
Apply SC2c decision (scope-out) — ROADMAP and TRIAGE.md updated
Human verification — full tests/pyspark/ suite clean

remaining_tasks.name: Fix test_pyspark_all_int_types: dtype string alias mismatch — 'int'/'integer' don't equal 'IntegerType()'. Need to resolve aliases before string comparison or use PySpark DataType.simpleString()/typeName() for normalization.

Fix test_pyspark_check_eq: error message format difference — narwhals produces "Check '<Check ...>' failed." but test expects "<Schema Column(...)> failed validation str_startswith('B')". Xfail the test for narwhals backend (message format is backend-specific).
Fix test_pyspark_dataframeschema: probably same dtype alias issue as 6a, investigate separately.

Fix TestEqualToCheck::test_failed_unaccepted_datatypes: narwhals backend raises PysparkSchemaError (catches AnalysisException as SCHEMA_COMPONENT_CHECK) but test expects TypeError. Xfail for narwhals backend.

blockers.description: Dtype alias mismatch: pandera schema Column('int') has schema.dtype.__str__() == 'int' but PySpark column has str(IntegerType()) == 'IntegerType()'. Need alias resolution before string comparison.
blockers.workaround: Use PySpark DataType.typeName() or compare against a normalized form. Or look up PySpark aliases table. The uncommitted check_dtype() in components.py uses str(pyspark_dtype) == str(schema.dtype) which is too strict.

blockers.description: Error message format difference: narwhals backend generates 'Check X failed.' while native PySpark generates '<Schema Column(...)> failed validation X'. Test_pyspark_check_eq asserts exact string.
blockers.workaround: Xfail test_pyspark_check_eq for narwhals backend with reason explaining the format difference.

decisions.decision: Do NOT add backend-specific if/is_pyspark branches in narwhals backend code
decisions.rationale: User explicitly rejected this pattern. Accept limitations or fix upstream/tests instead.

decisions.decision: SC2c: scope out row-index in failure_cases clause for PySpark
decisions.rationale: PySpark has no native integer row index (distributed)

decisions.decision: verifySchema=False removal was too broad — reverted. Only safe for fixtures with exact type matches.
decisions.rationale: Some test fixtures use float data for DecimalType columns which genuinely need verifySchema=False at createDataFrame time.

decisions.decision: run_schema_component_checks: use err.reason_code instead of SCHEMA_COMPONENT_CHECK
decisions.rationale: Value check failures (DATAFRAME_CHECK) should be DATA-level, not SCHEMA-level. Uncommitted fix in container.py.

next_action: Fix dtype alias issue in components.py check_dtype(): when schema.dtype is a string alias like 'int', resolve it to PySpark DataType before string comparison — use spark.createDataFrame schema field lookup or PySpark's _parse_datatype_string() to normalize. OR use schema.dtype.check() if it works for PySpark native types.
context_notes: Session discovered that the original Bug B diagnosis (concat_failure_cases) was partially wrong — the real issue for test_pyspark_check_eq is error categorization (SCHEMA_COMPONENT_CHECK vs DATAFRAME_CHECK). Both fixes are now uncommitted in container.py (reason_code fix) and base.py (fallback for empty pyspark_items). The dtype issue in components.py (check_dtype with is_pyspark branch) was rejected by user — the correct approach is to find a narwhals-native solution or accept the limitation. The current uncommitted check_dtype implementation uses string equality which fails for type aliases.
